name: Spumoni Slices
recipeIngredient: 1 Square (1 ounce) semi-sweet chocolate, melted and cooled
A few drops of green food coloring
Powdered sugar icing or Melted chocolate (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat shortening and margarine or butter for about 30 seconds or until softened.
2. Gradually add half the flour, the sugar, egg, 2 Tbsp milk, vanilla extract, baking soda, and 1/4 teaspoon salt to the mixture. Stir until thoroughly combined, scraping the sides of the bowl occasionally.
3. Divide the dough into three parts. Mix chocolate with 2 Tbsp of the milk in one portion; mix cherries in another; mix nuts, remaining milk, flavoring, and enough green food coloring to tint the dough in the third portion.
4. Shape the dough: Line an 8 x 4 x 2 inch loaf pan with clear plastic wrap. Press the pink dough evenly into the pan. Top with chocolate dough, then cover with green dough. Chill for 4 to 24 hours.
5. Invert the pan and remove the dough. Remove the plastic wrap. Cut the dough into 1/4-inch thick slices, then cut each slice crosswise into three pieces.
6. Place the pieces 2 inches apart on an ungreased cookie sheet. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 8 to 10 minutes or until the edges are lightly browned. Remove and cool on a rack.
7. Drizzle with powdered sugar icing tinted green or with melted chocolate.
Makes about 84 slices.
